studies.description: CT Chest / Abdomen / Pelvis with contrast — fever workup
studies.preview: Cirrhosis, ascites, patent TIPS, bilateral pleural effusions. NO drainable abscess or fluid collection anywhere; no bowel pathology; no occult source of infection identified. No mass/lesion to explain the weight loss within the imaged field.
Patent TIPS with appropriate shunt velocities. Confirms a functioning shunt after the emergent placement; the hemorrhage had stopped.
BILATERAL pleural effusions (right greater than left), consistent with hepatic hydrothorax. No focal consolidation. Heart size normal.

Coarse, nodular cirrhotic liver with splenomegaly and NEW moderate-volume ascites. Features of portal hypertension; portal vein patent. No focal liver mass identified on this study (dedicated HCC imaging had been planned outpatient).
